LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES SET ONE, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION SET ONE & FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. BY APRIL 12, 2013, MS. VOLTAGE SHALL SERVE COMPLETE VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO TRAIN CLAIM IS DENIED. FIRST, PLAINTIFF CORRECTLY NOTES AND DEFENDANT CONCEDES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT PROVIDE TIMELY NOTICE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(A). SECOND, THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE VIDEO AND IT DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THAT PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED NEGLIGENCE WAS THE SOLE CAUSE OF THE ACCIDENT. A REASONABLE TRIER OF FACT COULD CONCLUDE THAT THE OPERATOR DID NOT EXERCISE DUE CARE WHEN SHE LOWERED THE WHEELCHAIR RAMP. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-15-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication On Plaintiff'S Failure To Train Claim
Off Calendar Jun-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication On Plaintiff's Failure To Train Claim is denied. First, plaintiff correctly notes and defendant concedes that defendant did not provide timely notice in compliance with CCP 437c(a). Second, the court has reviewed the video and it does not demonstrate, as a matter of law, that plaintiff's alleged negligence was the sole cause of the accident. A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that the operator did not exercise due care when she lowered the wheelchair ramp. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Special Interrogatories Set One, And Request For Production Set One & For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-12-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Newton Oldfather, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4283, for defendant and moving party;
Sarine Voltage, Tel. 415.937.7420, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One, Special Interrogatories Set One, And Request For Production Set One & For Monetary Sanctions is granted. By April 12, 2013, Ms. Voltage shall serve complete verified responses, without objection, to City and County of San Francisco's Requests for Production of Documents. Request for attorney fees is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
